free_text: Experimental reevaluation of 235U fission yields at
the Egypt second research reactor (ETRR-2)
22 MW power reactor (ETRR-2)
Ten subsamples of natural UO2 (each 6.5 mg) were
mixed with about 6 mg Na2CO3 powder of Na as an
internal comparator. Triple bare flux monitors of
Au-Zr (99% pure) were prepared for neutron flux.
Two pure Na2CO3 samples used for investigate the
concentration of sodium in the compound using neutron
activation analysis and ~0.5 g of UO2 sample was
prepared for natural activity measurements.
Detector with relative efficiency of 100% and
2.1 keV energy resolution for 1332 keV gamma-line of
60Co line.
For short-lived the neutron flux was
3.787 X 10**12 n/cm2/sec and time of irradiation was
20 sec for each sample and 60 sec for flux monitor,
while for long-lived fission products the neutron flux
was (3.07-2.6) X 10**12 n/cm2/sec and irradiation time
was 2.5 hr.
Samples of 235U and 23Na are irradiated in the same
neutron flux, and the gamma activities are measured.
The thermal fission cross section of 235U and the
thermal capture cross section of 23Na are taken from
standard nuclear data libraries (ENDF/B-VIII.1,
JEFF-3.3, and JENDL-5) and averaged, with the
uncertainty taken as the standard deviation. Using the
Westcott method, with a factor of 1.001 for 23Na and
0.9803 +/- 0.0007 for 235U, the ratio of their
activities is calculated, and after applying decay
corrections, the fission yield from the 235U(n,f)
reaction is obtained.
Corrected for:
. Self-absorption
. Geometry effects
Uncertainty due to replicated measurements
Detector efficiency (1%)
Monte Carlo-based correction factors (<1%)
Proof-read by Nader M.A.Mohamed (2026-04-27)
Table IV and decay data from Supplemental material.
VT
